description: State held too high or non-const widgets cause the whole tree to rebuild on every change, producing list and animation jank.
body: Failure mode: all state lives in a top-level widget and subtrees are not const, so any small change rebuilds the entire tree including static parts, causing dropped frames on scrollable lists and animations. Detection signals: the DevTools rebuild counter shows far more widgets rebuilding than changed; jank during scrolling or typing; a single setState at the root driving the whole screen; widgets that never change lack const. Do not hoist all state to the top or omit const on static subtrees. Safer fallback: push state down to the smallest widget that needs it, mark unchanging subtrees const, use targeted listeners (Selector/ValueListenableBuilder) so only dependents rebuild, and confirm with the rebuild profiler.
